question: How many points does the race winner receive in the current Formula 1 scoring system?
last_answer: The race winner receives 25 points. [Source: The current points allocation in a Grand Prix is distributed as follows: the race winner receives 25 points, second place, 18, third, 15, fourth, 12, fifth, 10, sixth, 8, seventh, 6, eighth, 4, ninth, 2, and tenth, 1 point.]

question: Who has been responsible for supplying the Formula 1 safety car since 1995?
last_answer: Since 1995, Mercedes has been responsible for supplying the safety car [Source: Since 1995, Mercedes has been responsible for supplying this vehicle (with some exceptions such as the 1996 Argentine Grand Prix in which a Renault Clio was used)]. In 2021, Mercedes shares the supply of the safety car with Aston Martin [Source: In 2021 season, Mercedes shares the supply of the safety car with Aston Martin].

question: What does a yellow flag with red stripes indicate in Formula 1?
last_answer: A yellow flag with red stripes, also known as the striped flag, indicates the existence on the track of an element that causes a reduction in grip. [Source: The yellow flag with red stripes, also known as the striped flag, indicates the existence on the track of an element that causes a reduction in grip.] Drivers should reduce their speed at that point. [Source: Drivers should reduce their speed at that point.]

question: What was the greatest technological advance introduced by Lotus in 1962?
last_answer: Lotus introduced a car with an aluminium monocoque chassis instead of the traditional tubular frame in 1962. [Source: In 1962, Lotus introduced a car with an aluminium monocoque chassis instead of the traditional tubular frame. This proved to be the greatest technological advance since the introduction of rear-engined cars.]

question: What is the function of the virtual safety car in Formula 1?
last_answer: The virtual safety car is used to neutralise the race in cases where it is necessary to show a double yellow flag in some area of the track due to the existence of a risk for competitors or track marshals, without the incident requiring the appearance of the safety car. [Source: In 2015, the use of the virtual safety car began to be implemented. It is used to neutralise the race in cases where it is necessary to show a double yellow flag in some area of the track due to the existence of a risk for competitors or track marshals, without the incident requiring the appearance of the safety car.] When race control initiates the virtual safety car procedure, all luminous panels around the circuit will display the letters VSC and the teams will be notified. [Source: When race control initiates the virtual safety car procedure, all luminous panels around the circuit will display the letters VSC and the teams will be notified.] Drivers must respect a minimum time established by the FIA at each control post. [Source: Drivers must respect a minimum time established by the FIA at least once at each control post.]

question: How many points did first place receive in the 1950-1959 scoring system compared to the current system?
last_answer: In the 1950-1959 scoring system, first place received 8 points [Source: From 1950 to 1959, first place received 8 points, second place 6 points, third place 4 points, fourth place 3 points, fifth place 2 points, and an additional 1 point was awarded for the fastest lap of the race.]. Currently, first place receives 25 points [Source: the race winner receives 25 points, second place, 18, third, 15, fourth, 12, fifth, 10, sixth, 8, seventh, 6, eighth, 4, ninth, 2, and tenth, 1 point.].

last_answer: In the early eras, championships averaged around 10-11 Grand Prix. question: How did the engine regulations change in 2014 compared to the previous era?
last_answer: In 2014, Formula 1 underwent a major regulatory change, replacing V8 engines with turbocharged 1.6-litre V6 units. [Source: In 2014, Formula 1 underwent a major regulatory change, replacing V8 engines with turbocharged 1.6-litre V6 units.] The single-seaters now feature a hybrid power unit, adding an energy recovery system. [Source: The single-seaters now feature a hybrid power unit, adding an energy recovery system.]

question: Which driver and team dominated Formula 1 the longest consecutive period and during which years?
last_answer: Michael Schumacher and Ferrari dominated from 2000 to 2004, winning five consecutive drivers championships and six constructors championships. [Source: Schumacher and Ferrari won five consecutive drivers championships (2000-2004) and six constructors championships (1999-2004), something without precedent.]

question: What was the combined impact of the 2017 regulatory changes on Formula 1 cars?
last_answer: The single-seaters became the fastest in history. [Source: The engine development and the 2017 regulatory change, which improved aerodynamic downforce, resulted in the single-seaters becoming the fastest in history.]
